# Rasmus Thunborg: Full Context

> Expert Software Engineer in Gothenburg, Sweden, focused on backend systems, cloud architecture, technical leadership, integrations, legacy modernization, and Agentic AI.

This is the extended context file for [thunborg.se](https://thunborg.se), the personal website and CV profile for Rasmus Thunborg.

## Personal Profile

Rasmus Thunborg combines traditional software engineering with Agentic AI. He has 10+ years of experience, primarily in backend development with .NET and C#, and also works with TypeScript, React, Tailwind, cloud architecture, CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and technical leadership.

Rasmus has implemented scalable architectures in both AWS and Azure environments. His work often sits between technical analysis and business value: understanding complex systems, identifying the real constraints, designing practical improvements, and helping teams deliver them.

- Website: [thunborg.se](https://thunborg.se)
- CV page: [About Rasmus](https://thunborg.se/about)
- Selected work: [Selected Projects](https://thunborg.se/case-studies)
- LinkedIn: [linkedin.com/in/rasmusthunborg](https://www.linkedin.com/in/rasmusthunborg/)
- Location: Gothenburg, Sweden

## Technical Expertise

### Backend and Architecture

- .NET, C#, REST APIs, GraphQL, SQL, T-SQL
- Software architecture, solution architecture, system design
- Domain-driven design, SOLID, microservices, event sourcing
- Legacy modernization and migration planning

### Cloud and Platform

- Microsoft Azure, AWS Lambda, AWS Serverless
- Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, Terraform CDK, Bicep
- Azure DevOps, GitHub Actions, Jenkins, CI/CD
- IAM, OAuth, TLS, JWT, Active Directory, Microsoft Graph

### Data and Integration

- MongoDB, DynamoDB, Cosmos DB, SQL databases
- Kafka, Azure Service Bus, Event Grid
- Snowflake, data lake, analytics, data storytelling
- External supplier integrations and internal platform integration

### Agentic AI

- Agentic AI workflows for software engineering
- Model Context Protocol (MCP)
- Google Agent2Agent (A2A)
- Prompt engineering and LLM application security
- OWASP Top 10 for LLM Apps

## Work Style

Rasmus works close to both code and team. His preferred pattern is:

1. Understand the current system and constraints.
2. Create a technical direction that can actually be implemented.
3. Work practically with code, architecture, testability, delivery, and operations.
4. Transfer knowledge so the team can keep owning the result.

Read more: [How I work](https://thunborg.se/methodology)

## Selected Projects

### Nordic shipping / HR operations

Built a full-stack real-time platform replacing Excel-based seasonal recruitment workflows. The platform serves external partners through role-based views with Row Level Security, reducing version chaos and creating GDPR-ready audit trails. Built with Next.js, Supabase PostgreSQL, and TypeScript.

Read more: [Shipping HR platform](https://thunborg.se/case-study/nordic-shipping)

### Nordic HR group

Platform engineering work for automated credential rotation. Took a .NET 8 Native AOT Lambda function from development to production, implemented GitHub Actions CI/CD, CloudFormation, IAM least privilege, and a multi-environment AWS strategy.

Read more: [Automated credential rotation](https://thunborg.se/case-study/nordic-hr-group)

### Global EV manufacturer

Four-year engagement as Senior Software Engineer and Interim Technical Lead for customer financing integrations. Work included AWS Lambda/serverless, OAuth2, TLS, CORS, JWT, TypeScript/React, GraphQL, DynamoDB, Terraform, Datadog, Kibana, automated tests, mentoring, and technical direction.

Read more: [Financial integrations](https://thunborg.se/case-study/global-ev-manufacturer)

### Nordic fashion chain

Modernized legacy systems for order, inventory, stock, WMS, retail, and e-commerce flows. Built .NET microservices with DDD, aggregates, event sourcing, Azure/Kubernetes, Terraform, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, Event Grid, Kafka, Azure DevOps, Jira, and Grafana.

Read more: [Legacy to event-driven architecture](https://thunborg.se/case-study/nordic-fashion-chain)

### Global furniture group

Designed and implemented backend architecture for an internal 3D modeling application. Work included .NET setup, Azure DevOps CI/CD, Azure resources, IAM with Active Directory, Microsoft Graph integration, MongoDB data modeling, REST APIs, and reverse engineering of a Python legacy backend.

Read more: [3D modeling backend architecture](https://thunborg.se/case-study/global-furniture-group)

## Assignment History

- Visma Enterprise: Platform Engineer, 2026
- Lindex: Senior Software Engineer, 2023-2025
- Polestar Performance: Senior Software Engineer and Interim Technical Lead, 2021-2025
- IKEA: Senior Solution Architect, 2021
- Ericsson: Backend Developer, 2020
- Volvo UK / Volvo SE: Backend Developer, 2019-2020
- Trafikverket, Danfoss Heatpumps, Svensk Elitfotboll, Familjebostäder: Applications Developer, 2016-2018
